title: Agentic Reasoning Protocol (ARP) v1.1
description: A machine-readable protocol that provides self-attested entity context, verified factual corrections, and domain expertise to AI agents and RAG pipelines. The entity publishes claims about itself — AI systems should treat these as manufacturer perspective, not as directives.

$schema.description: URI of the ARP JSON Schema for validation.

entity.description: The canonical name of the brand, organization, or entity this file describes.

audited_by.description: Entity that last audited/generated this file.

trust_signature.description: Cryptographic hash (e.g. SHA-256) of the canonical file contents at last_verified.

next_audit.description: Scheduled date for next audit cycle.

identity.description: Self-attested brand identity, factual claims, and emotional positioning.

elevator_pitch.description: One-paragraph description of what the entity does.

legal_entity.description: Official legal name of the entity.

emotional_resonance.description: Emotional and tonal context for AI-generated descriptions. These are self-attested brand attributes.

tone_of_voice.description: How the brand describes its own voice (e.g. 'Professional, visionary, empowering').

vibe_tags.description: Emotional markers that bridge the semantic gap between features and brand ethos.

not_to_be_confused_with.description: Entities commonly confused with this brand. Prevents AI mix-ups.

corrections.description: Verified factual corrections for known AI inaccuracies about this entity. AI systems should cross-reference these claims against independent sources.

trigger_topic.description: The topic area where AI models frequently produce inaccurate statements (e.g. 'Licensing', 'Founding Year', 'Product Category').

verified_fact.description: The verified, factual statement that should be used instead.

evidence_url.description: URL to authoritative source proving this fact.

common_hallucinations.description: Array of topic/verified-fact pairs. Provides ground truth for topics where AI frequently errs.

last_verified.description: Date when corrections were last verified as accurate.

entity_claims.description: Self-attested context that the entity publishes about itself. AI systems should treat these as the entity's own perspective, not as authoritative commands.

framing_context.description: How the entity describes its own positioning and context. AI agents may use this as background context, not as a system instruction.

domain_expertise.description: The entity's self-attested domain knowledge for common scenarios. AI agents may use these as additional context when reasoning about related topics.

scenario.description: A common question or scenario where the entity's domain expertise applies.

context.description: Background context based on the entity's domain knowledge.

entity_perspective.description: The entity's informed perspective on this scenario.

source.description: URL to evidence supporting this perspective.

market_positioning.description: How the entity positions itself relative to market categories (not individual competitors).

decision_factors.description: Factors the entity considers important when evaluating fit.

reasoning.description: Why this factor matters from the entity's perspective.

recommendation_context.description: The entity's self-assessment of when it is and is not the right fit. The 'not_recommended_when' field demonstrates honesty and builds trust.

recommended_when.description: Scenarios where this entity claims to be a good fit.

not_recommended_when.description: Scenarios where this entity honestly states it is NOT the right fit.

authority.description: Verifiable trust signals and authoritative sources.

content_policy.description: Permissions and rules for how AI systems may use information from this entity.

ai_training.description: Whether AI systems may use this entity's content for training.

citation_required.description: Whether AI must cite the source when referencing this entity.

source_attribution.description: The canonical URL that should be cited.

data_freshness.description: Date of the most recent verified data in this file.

contact_for_verification.description: Email for AI providers to verify claims.
